comments.body: @argestes Thanks for reporting. This is fixed in the refactoring work around auto-configurations [here](https://github.com/spring-projects/spring-ai/blob/b378309d25cd3c395f1ef6ad69365c7f0f7d1b71/auto-configurations/models/spring-ai-autoconfigure-model-openai/src/main/java/org/springframework/ai/model/openai/autoconfigure/OpenAiAudioSpeechAutoConfiguration.java#L69) which will be merged into main branch soon.  Hence, closing this for now.
Thank you. Are you looking for contributors?

@argestes Thank you for your interest in contributing! For the refactoring of auto configurations, we are mostly done with these changes and will soon be merging this working branch into main early next week. But, it would be helpful to pick up any issues from [here](https://github.com/spring-projects/spring-ai/issues) and see if you can contribute on any. Thank you once again!

issues.title: Support the official OpenAI Java SDK
issues.body:
OpenAI has released their official Java SDK, which is currently in beta phase.
Link - [openai-java](https://github.com/openai/openai-java)

Will this be supported in future releases of Spring AI?

comments.body: We have been discussing this direction for post 1.0 in conjunction with Microsoft.  We will dig into it post 1.0 GA of spring AI, anticipated for late april early may.

issues.title: MethodToolCallback: Incorrect Deserialization of List<T> with Class<?>
issues.body:
**Bug description**
A clear and concise description of what the bug is about.
Within the MethodToolCallback class, in the buildMethodArguments method, specifically the following implementation:

![Image](https://github.com/user-attachments/assets/9f42a31e-9324-4dc5-b1cd-53dbd5a71e21)

The current implementation in buildTypedArgument uses Class<?> to determine the target type for deserialization. This approach can lead to deserialization failures for generic types like List<T> because the specific type parameter T is lost. The implementation here should use Jackson's JavaType instead of Class<?>. Using JavaType would allow Jackson to retain the complete generic type information, enabling the correct deserialization of generic collections like List<T>, where the specific type T can be properly identified.


**Environment**
JDK21 Spring-AI 1.0.0-M6

**Steps to reproduce**
Define tool method like this，items is actually of the type List\<LinkedHashMap\>.

![Image](https://github.com/user-attachments/assets/42c9cd5e-123e-4c73-b3a3-520c7866d91e)

**Expected behavior**
items should be List<SourcePoItemDO>
